level_signals: Staff title with end-to-end roadmap ownership
Expected to drive technical direction alongside engineering leads
Base pay Zone 1: $191,000–$225,000 USD, consistent with Staff-level IC

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 7+ years of consumer-facing product management, with 3+ years on AI/ML-powered products
7+ years of consumer-facing product management, with 3+ years on AI/ML-powered products

AI Architecture & Technical Strategy — Deep fluency in LLM architectures, agentic systems, and retrieval-augmented generation; able to hold strong, informed opinions on build-vs-buy decisions, model selection, and system design alongside engineering leaders

Industry Pace-Setting — Track record of translating rapid advances in AI research and tooling into product roadmap decisions, ensuring the product stays at or ahead of the frontier rather than reacting to it

Evaluation & Continuous Improvement — Experience building and evolving evaluation frameworks that measure AI quality, accuracy, and safety at scale; able to establish systematic feedback loops and quality bars that enable the team to ship with confidence and improve continuously

Consumer-Obsessed AI Product Craft — Ability to identify high-impact, user-facing improvements across a full AI product experience and ship them iteratively, turning a complex AI system into an intuitive financial companion customers trust and rely on daily
Strong sense of urgency, high ownership, and comfort operating in ambiguity across a fast-moving technical and regulatory landscape
Bachelor's degree, Master's, or equivalent experience

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience shipping AI products in financial services or other regulated industries
Familiarity with LLM evaluation and observability tooling
Experience with agentic AI systems in production
Background in financial advice, portfolio management, or wealth-tech products

scope.surface: Cortex — Robinhood's consumer-facing AI for investment research, portfolio analysis, financial planning, and personalized advice
primary_metrics_inferred: Customer adoption and retention of Cortex
AI quality, accuracy, and safety scores
Progression from research assistant to AI-powered financial planning
core_responsibilities: Own the Cortex product roadmap end-to-end — from improving the core conversational experience today to defining the path toward AI-powered financial planning and advice
Drive the technical direction alongside engineering leads — making high-conviction decisions on AI architecture, model strategy, and system design that keep Cortex at the frontier of what's possible
Build and evolve evaluation frameworks at scale — establish the automated quality, accuracy, and safety measurement systems that let the team ship with confidence and improve continuously
Ship consumer experiences that drive adoption and retention — find the moments where AI creates real value in customers' financial lives and deliver them with craft and speed
Navigate the regulatory landscape — partner with Legal & Compliance to responsibly expand what Cortex can do, from neutral research toward personalized financial guidance
Lead cross-functional execution across engineering, design, data science, compliance, and operations — turning ambiguity into shipped product

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed across multiple zones ($191K–$225K base for Zone 1)
Equity and bonus explicitly mentioned in addition to base
Clear product surface and ownership scope (Cortex AI)
Strong mission statement with quantified wealth transfer context ($124 trillion)
Comprehensive benefits including 100% paid health insurance
Role is on a high-visibility, strategically important product within Robinhood
red_flags: Hybrid policy requires at least 3 days per week in Menlo Park, CA or New York City, NY — eliminates remote candidates
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not addressed in JD
Regulatory constraints on financial advice products may slow roadmap execution
Several nice-to-haves are listed inline with must-haves without clear separation, making hard/soft distinction ambiguous

ambiguities: The 'What You Bring' section lists 'Experience shipping AI products in financial services or other regulated industries', 'Familiarity with LLM evaluation and observability tooling', 'Experience with agentic AI systems in production', and 'Background in financial advice, portfolio management, or wealth-tech products' without explicit 'preferred' or 'nice to have' language. These appear after a series of clearly required competencies and are treated as nice-to-haves based on their more junior/supplemental framing and placement.

The JD names Zone 2 and Zone 3 city compensation tiers (Denver, Westlake TX, Chicago, Lake Mary FL, Clearwater FL, Gainesville FL) but the role is only based in Menlo Park, CA or New York, NY. These additional zones likely apply to other roles or remote-eligible employees; Zone 1 is used as the primary compensation range.
No explicit mention of visa sponsorship availability or work authorization requirements.
